review.summary: A user interface function that prompts the user to select between two AI models and returns the corresponding provider name.

issues.description: Model name 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514' appears to be incorrect or non-existent - Claude models don't follow this naming pattern
issues.fix: Use correct Claude model name like 'claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022' or similar valid model identifier

review.suggestions: Consider adding type hints for the return value. The function could be more flexible by accepting model configurations as parameters rather than hardcoding them. Consider adding docstring to document the function's purpose and return values.
review.rating_reason: Code works correctly for its purpose but contains an incorrect model name and could benefit from better documentation and flexibility.

code:
async function submitReview() { const code = document.getElementById("code-input").value const model = document.querySelector('input[name="model"]:checked').value const response = await fetch("/review", { method: "POST", headers: {"Content-Type": "application/json"}, body: JSON.stringify({code: code, model: model}) }) const result = await response.json() const issueHTML = result.issues.map(issue => `
  • Line ${issue.line}: ${issue.description} (${issue.severity})
    • `).join("") document.getElementById("result-area").innerHTML = `

    Summary

    ${result.summary}

    Rating

    ${result.rating} - ${result.rating_reason}

    Suggestions

    ${result.suggestions}

    Issues

      ${issueHTML}
    ` }

review.summary: JavaScript function that submits code for review to a server endpoint and displays the formatted results in the DOM.

issues.description: This is JavaScript code, not Python as indicated in the request
issues.fix: Clarify the programming language or provide actual Python code

issues.description: No error handling for missing DOM elements - will throw if elements don't exist
issues.fix: Add null checks: if (!codeElement) throw new Error('Code input not found')

issues.description: No error handling for missing radio button selection - will throw if no model is selected
issues.fix: Add null check: const modelElement = document.querySelector(...); if (!modelElement) throw new Error('No model selected')

issues.description: No error handling for network failures or non-200 responses
issues.fix: Add try-catch block and check response.ok before parsing JSON

issues.description: No error handling if response.json() fails or result structure is invalid
issues.fix: Add validation for expected response structure before accessing properties

issues.description: Potential XSS vulnerability - directly inserting user content without sanitization
issues.fix: Use textContent instead of innerHTML for dynamic content, or sanitize HTML

review.suggestions: Add comprehensive error handling with try-catch blocks. Validate DOM element existence before use. Sanitize all dynamic content to prevent XSS attacks. Consider adding loading states and user feedback during the async operation. Add input validation before sending the request.
review.rating_reason: Functional code but lacks error handling and has security vulnerabilities that could cause runtime errors or XSS attacks
